boardgame: Salo Helenica Juego de Mesa Mitos y Leyendas
description:
Board game adaptation of the entire classic Helenica set of Mitos y Leyendas (Myths and Legends CCG)

This limited edition game has all necessary accessories (playmats, coin, hourglass) to have a 2, 3 or 4 player game based on the 20 year old strategy collectible card game. This version has all 340 cards from the Helenica era.

boardgame: Salo Espada Sagrada Juego de Mesa Mitos y Leyendas
description:
Board game adaptation of the entire classic Espada Sagrada set of Mitos y Leyendas (Myths and Legends CCG)

This limited edition game has all necessary accessories (playmats, coin, hourglass) to have a 2, 3 or 4 player game based on the 20 year old strategy collectible card game. This version has all 340 cards from the Espada Sagrada era.

boardgame: Salo Dominios de Ra Juego de Mesa Mitos y Leyendas
description:
Board game adaptation of the entire classic Dominios de Ra set of Mitos y Leyendas (Myths and Legends CCG)

This limited edition game has all necessary accessories (playmats, coin, hourglass) to have a 2, 3 or 4 player game based on the 20 year old strategy collectible card game. This version has all 340 cards from the Dominios de Ra era.

Crashing Waves is the final expansion for Dicetopia that introduces a new unsettling dock area with three new locations adding more strategies and possibilities! Visit the Crashing Waves Stock Exchange to manipulate the value of dice resources. Break out a fellow thug from Cutthroat Prison for an extra turn and a stronger area control presence. Or drop by the Molotov Market to trade dice values!

You’ll also find more fishy factions, a rebalance of the original city board, as well as full support for an extra player, making Dicetopia go up to whopping 6 players together with the Roll with the Punches Expansion!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Age of Steam Deluxe Expansion: Detroit Bankruptcy & Heavy Cardboard Maps
description: Includes: a 2 sided game board with the Detroit Bankruptcy and Heavy Cardboard maps in the new Age of Steam: Deluxe graphic style.

You are the manager of a futuristic vehicle racing team and must win the hearts of the public assisted by your mechanic robots and your pilot. It is a workers placement with race management and vehicle improvement while managing the chaos of combat between players.

In a turn, you have 2 actions. You must move your vehicle and your second action may be to place a worker, make another movement or use your weapon.

The workers have various possibilities like going to get parts to improve your vehicle, entertaining the crowd to increase your glory, repairing your vehicle, stealing parts from other players and many others.

There is a system for hitting between vehicles and hitting robotic workers on the track.

The game ends when the first player finishes his third turn on the track. After, each player plays his last round.

The final scoring is done by counting the points of glory accumulated in each round, end of game objectives, each point not accumulated counts for half and finally, each 2 pieces count for 1 point.

There are several mechanics in this game. There is a part making charter. Glory points are accumulated by interacting with other players and you must also manage the race and the workers placement.

—description from the designer

Find three matching words as quickly as possible for a category and two specific letters. But be careful: If your teammates have written down the same words, you will unfortunately have to delete them and you will not get any points!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Somewhere In The Heavens…They Are Waiting
description:
Explore the heavens for technology that will destroy the asteroid called “Wormwood” before it reaches Earth.

Somewhere In The Heavens… They Are Waiting is a fully cooperative, action-adventure experience based on the beloved Mac computer game "Marathon", for 2 to 6 players.

It’s the year 2155 and a large asteroid is headed toward Earth. It’s estimated that the asteroid, dubbed Wormwood, will reach earth’s atmosphere in 24 days. Meanwhile, you are an officer aboard the Gryphon, an AI controlled space cruiser, when your employer alerts you of your next mission. It sounds simple enough. A standard security check on an abandoned nuclear power facility. You will slowly uncover truths that connect to the fast approaching artifact.

Players will use an action point system to navigate through space finding the necessary technologies needed to stop the asteroid. One player will play as the ship A.I., Gabriel, using a web app to guide the 1-5 Officer players on planetary missions to accomplish the goals necessary to win. Officer players will acquire suit armor upgrades and weapons upgrades to fight against the threats attempting to keep the asteroid on its course. Missions will take you through space to industrial facilities, military strongholds, and alien architecture.

—description from the designer

OMEGA is a strategic game, revisiting in an original way the theme of secret societies.
You play as an apprentice who is trying to join a mysterious organization known as OOTO. For this, you will have to summon the Ancient Spirits to discover the Arcana of the Omega. So, when the ritual ends, the Disciples will decide which apprentice is worthy to join them...

In a Game of OMEGA, you engage in an occult ritual at the end of which will
be designated the new Initiate of the brotherhood. You will summon Spirits with Runes. These Spirits will guide your progress on the Ritual disc in order to access the Arcana.
The first candidate to reach the number 7 Arcana will end the Ritual. Spirits, Runes and Arcana have special powers. You will have to learn to handle them and combine them to hope to be chosen.
There will be many strategies that can lead you to victory, but when you appear before the judges, be sure to have by your side powerful Arcana and, if possible, enough runes to best satisfy each of your Spirits...

(Note: This listing covers only the compilation versions of The Lost Expedition that include both the base game and the expansion, plus all (currently) released promos; for a listing of the expansion-only versions, go to The Lost Expedition: The Fountain of Youth & Other Adventures).

Legendary explorer Percy Fawcett marched deep into the Amazon in search of El Dorado. He was never seen again. Your team has gone in search of him, but now you hope to escape the jungle with the greatest treasure of all: your life.

Make the best of your food, your ammunition, and your health in The Lost Expedition as you plunge deep into the jungle. Choose your path carefully to ensure you're ready for the pitfalls that may occur. Play solo or cooperatively to survive the expedition, or play in teams to see which group can escape the jungle first.

The Lost Expedition: The Fountain of Youth & Other Adventures expands the base game with four new, modular additions.

In The Fountain of Youth & Other Adventures, Francisco de Orellana and his band of conquistadors traveled deep into the Amazon and were never seen again. Centuries later, unbelievable reports of men clad in ancient armor armed with sword and arquebus have surfaced. If the rumors are true, de Orellana found the Fountain of Youth and guards it dearly...

Other reports have emerged from the area of a mysterious mountain deep in the jungle, pulling planes crashing down from the sky. There are whispers, too, of a dark curse that the locals call only the Mark. For those foolhardy enough to still journey into the depths of the continent, they best not travel alone.

—description from the publisher

A party game where you roast your friends.

BOOM! Roasted begins with each player drawing 7 cards from the top of the deck in the “Roasts” pile. The youngest player in the group assumes the role of the Honoree and gets the first verbal beating—take your medicine and shut up about it!

The remaining players serve as the Roasters for the round, while the Honoree draws a “Setups” card and reads the set-up aloud beginning with their own name, and ending with “Roast Me.” Each Roaster then chooses the “Roasts” card that best corresponds with the setup and hands it face-down to the Honoree, who then shuffles the responses.

After shuffling, the Honoree inserts their first name while re-reading aloud the “Setups” card in conjunction with each of the “Roasts” cards, and chooses the funniest or most clever combination. The Roaster responsible for the best burn must point their finger at the Honoree and say, “Boom! Roasted” and is awarded the Setups card, which counts as one point.

Twofers: On “Setups” card scenarios requiring two answers, place the “Roasts” card for your first answer on the bottom with the second answer on top so that it may be read by the Honoree in the order you intended.

Roasters will replace any card(s) used during the previous round; the Honoree from the previous round is now free to roast the shit out of the next person.

The person to the Honoree’s left is the next in-line to be trashed by their friends. The Roaster with the most “Setups” cards/points at the end of the game wins the coveted title of Roastmaster or Roastmistress.

This game is intended for entertainment purposes only. BOOM! Roasted is not responsible for any depression, lack of self-worth, wallowing pity, OR any therapist expenses incurred as a result of playing this game.

If you don’t know what a word in our instructions or deck of cards means, you must be the type of person who doesn’t know how to Google things.

Note: If you’re already an asshole before playing this game, our material is not intended to serve as a form of realistic hazing or degradation. In other words, don’t be a bullying dickhead to people in real life.

—description from the designer

Will you be the most skilled castle builder in the kingdom?
Place room cards to grow your castle, but be careful!
The other builders could use dragons to block your construction!!!

PURPOSE OF THE GAME 
Be the most skilled castle builder in the kingdom. You will have to place the room cards of the castle by matching the stairs and the corridors of entry with those of exit of the previous card placed on the left.
The player who manages to put 10 room cards in a row wins first.

PREPARATION AND DEVELOPMENT 
For 4 players you have to use all 64 cards
For 2-3 players you have to remove 2 Dragons and 2 Knights of your choice

Shuffle the cards and deal 2 face down to each player, the remaining cards are placed in the center of the playing area to create the draw deck.
The player who last visited a castle begins the game and continues clockwise. During his turn, eachplayer draws 2 cards to add to his hand and creates his own row or row of room cards in front of him starting from the left and adding other cards only to the right of the previous one. Each player can play as many cards as he wants from his hand, the important thing is that to pass the turn he does not have more than 2 cards in his hand. If you have 3 or more cards in your hand you cannot pass the turn but you will have to play them until you remain with 2 cards or less. WARNING: if you have 3 or more DRAGON or KNIGHT cards in your hand and you cannot use them, they will be discarded until you have 2 cards in your hand.

CREATING A ROW OF ROOMS
The rows of rooms are created starting from the first card on the left and adding the other cards always to the right of the previous one, by matching the stairs and the entrance corridors with those of exit of the previous card, it is not possible to add cards to the left of the row. All room cards have two identical symbols, positioned on the top and bottom sides and are an HELMET and a couple of CROSSED SWORDS. When building a row of rooms, it is mandatory that all symbols face the same direction, therefore, if you start a row with the HELMET facing up, all the cards that make up the row must have the HELMETS facing up. If you have no cards in your hand that can be added to a row that you are building and you have more than 2 cards in your hand, then you cannot pass the turn, you will have to start a new row, always matching HELMETS and CROSSED SWORDS, therefore, if your first row has the HELMET symbol facing upwards, the row that you will build must have the same symbol facing downwards. There is no limit to the number of rows of rooms you can build.

USING DRAGON CARDS AND KNIGHT CARDS
DRAGON cards can be used against an opponent, during your turn they can be placed to the right of a row of rooms to block the construction, because from this moment the opponent cannot add ROOM CARDS on his own row. The KNIGHT cards instead must be used on their own row of rooms where a DRAGON card has been placed to remove it and be able to continue building on that row. Once played it must be discarded together with the removed DRAGON card, these cards can no longer be used.

WINNING THE GAME
The player who first creates a row of 10 ROOM cards wins the game. If the cards in the draw deck finish,and no one had made a 10-card row, in this case the player who made the longest row of rooms wins, if there is an equal merit, whoever has made the second longest row, if there is still a tie, will check the third and so on.

—description from the publisher

Card game and strategy for 2 - 4 players from 8 years of age in which each player must complete their DREAM by creating a row of 6 cards with increasing numbers from 1 to 6, hindering opponents with the NIGHTMARE cards.
Can you complete your dream and make your opponents have nightmares?

OBJECTIVE OF GAME
Complete your Dream row by trying to obstruct your opponents with Nightmare cards.
The winner is the player who creates a row of 6 Dream cards, with an increasing numbering from 1 to 6, without holding any cards in hand.

SPECIAL CASES
When a player no longer holds any cards and there are no more cards on the deck, but in his row there are also Nightmare cards, the opponents can continue playing until they hold no cards in hand. The player with most Dream cards in his row wins.
In the event of a tie the winner is the one who has the most consecutive Dream cards in his row.

PREPARATION 
Shuffle the deck and deal out 4 cards to each player. The deck is then left in the centre of game area. The youngest player starts and the game proceeds clockwise.

PROCEDURE
During the game each player must choose between:
Taking one card from the deck and putting it in his hand 
Playing a card in his own row or in the row of an opponent
Players can hold up to 10 cards in hand.

—description from the publisher

Bodyguard is a card game in which a president, a bodyguard and  any number of assassins try to reach their goals over the course of several rounds. In each round, a different player plays the president.

The president wins by eliminating any assassin (or surviving by the end of the round).
The assassins win if they kill the president.
The bodyguard wins by rescuing the president.

The president deals the role cards to the other players, with one card remaining unassigned. Also, each player holds six hand cards. On a turn, a player can either play an action or a hit card. A player who has four different hit cards in front of them, is eliminated from the round (typically meaning the end of the round, unless the bodyguard is eliminated first). If the president receives the fourth hit card, the bodyguard can play three different hit cards to rescue the president. Afterwards, points are awarded and another round start, until each player has played the president once. Highest overall score wins the game.

Each player sets his 4 pawns on the starting spaces (1-4) of his color. Movement is determined by a die. On the roll of a 6 a pawn may be moved from a starting space. On spaces 1-9 pawns of the same color may not pass each other by.

On the red spaces pawns may be thrown out if a pawn of another color is arriving. Thrown out pawns are set on space 9 and the player has to pay 1 coin into the jackpot (2 coins if his pawn already stands on space 9). Pawns on yellow spaces are safe, but their player has to pay 1 coin to another player arriving with a pawn on the same space. Pawns of the same color may never stand on the same space, expect for space 9.

The first player to get his 4 pawns around the game board and back to spaces 1-4 wins the game and the jackpot.

—user summary

Each player has an starting amount of 12 points and choses one of the available starting spaces. Movement is determined by a die.

If a player gets on a numbered space, he refers to the corresponding number near the border of the game board and follows the rules printed there. As a result he may lose or gain points.

If a player reaches an occupied space, he has to draw a trivia card About the history of Israel and answer the question printed there. If he answers correctly, he may advance 5 spaces. If he does'nt know the answer he has to go 10 spaces back and loses 10 points.

The first player to reach space 50 (Jerusalem) wins the game and is proclaimed major of Jerusalem. The player with the most points gets to be prime minister of Israel.

—user summary

boardgame: A Elegia do Pássaro da Morte
description:
A Elegia do Pássaro da Morte (The Elegy of the Bird of Death) is an eleven card microgame. 6 double-sided cards contain verses of a poem, 4 are identity cards, and the last one is the death card.

The verse cards are shuffled and laid out in a column, forming a poem. Each player receives a character card. The poem tells about the characters' death, and the goal of the game is to manipulate the poem and escape death 4 times.

Each character card shows four combinations of verse card symbols, one combinatino on each side. Whenever each of these appears in the poem, all turned to the red card side, the player has escaped once, and then they turn their character card by 90 degrees to achieve the next sequence.

On a turn, can do three actions: they may move one of the last two poem cards to the first position in the poem, or flip the first card of the poem to the other side (which results in certain other cards to be flipped as well), or escape death by having the three necessary cards flipped to the red side.

The first player to escape death four times is the winner - everybody else dies.

The game is based on the novel O Réquiem do Pássaro da Morte by Andrio Santos. Although the poem is in Portuguese, the game itself is language independent.

The Depressing Game is a social deduction game, where players take on the role of high school students who have to deal with depression on a daily basis

This game was designed by the students of Nanakuli High School, located on the west coast of O'ahu, Hawaii.

While understanding the serious of the topic, this card game is light and humorous, and uses satire to explore the sensitive subject.

Gameplay summary:

You are high school students interacting on campus. The happy players are attempting to find out who is the depressed player and bring them around to the realm of sunshine. The depressed player is attempting to infect the other players with a contagion of woe.

A the beginning of the game platers are dealt a hidden role.


     If you are secretly playing a Happy Role (weirdo) the last round must be won by a Happy Card.
     If you are secretly playing the Depressed Role (I feel for you) the last round must be won by a Depressing Card DR the last round ends in a tie.


On a turn:

     Each player selects 1 card from their hand and places it face down ... (thinking about rainbows)
     All players then say "HAPPY!" and reveal their cards simultaneously.
     A player wins the round and furthers their secret agenda by having the highest numeric value of all cards played. Tied numbers cancel each other out. 


The player who plays the highest number wins the round. All tied numbers cancel out and count as zero.

The winner of the round selects 1 action (A or B)

a)Take a card from the Tableau of Emotions (cards in the center of the table). Now take a card from your hand and place that card face up in the Tableau of Emotions.

b)Take a card from the Tableau of Emotions(cards in the center of the table). Now take a random card from another player's hand and place that card face up in the Tableau of Emotions.

The winner will gather all the cards just played. place them together face-down in a "Discard pile" and place that pile aside. Place all played cards in the Discard pile from now on. No players may look at the discarded cards during the game.

Continue playing cards round after round until each player is down to their last card. This is the round that counts for the game.

All players play their last card face down. Before flipping over their last card, all players reveal their Hidden Role.

Players flip over their last cards following the same rules. Except the winning card's type (Happy or Depressed) determines the winner.

—description from the designer

Join the Locke family and their friends as they explore landmarks in their new home of Lovecraft, MA. Find and use powerful keys, make powerful allies, and put together the pieces of the past. But beware - not everyone is who they appear to be! Demons may be hiding among the group, enacting their own devious plans. Shadow of Doubt is a race to root out the traitors before they can open the Black Door and unleash a horde of demons upon the world!

Based on the award-winning graphic novel Locke & Key by IDW Publishing, Shadow of Doubt is a light, snappy social deduction game that's dripping with theme.

—description from the publisher

A Todo Vapor! (Full Steam!) is a party game which is played while engaging in other activities. Each player draws a character card that is either a suspect or an investigator. The suspects have a list of activities that they have to fulfill, while each investigator has two special abilities that they can each use once during the game.

Whenever an investigator believes that a suspect is doing an activity from their list, they can call out the suspect. If correct, the suspect may no longer use that activity to score. If wrong, the investigator loses one of their special abilities.

The suspects win if any of them manages to execute three of their activities. The investigators win if none of the suspects has enough undetected activities left.

This game is played with a standard 52 card deck, either solo as a simulation, or a two-player game where two teams compete against each other in a simulated cricket match.

A coin flip decides who bats or bowls first and play begins by drawing six cards. The aim is to play a card that's higher than your opponents.

If the bowler's card is higher,  a dot ball or a wicket happens. If the batter's card is higher, the batter scores that many runs or causes a wicket. Otherwise if tied, there is an automatic dot ball.

The innings ends after a predetermined amount of overs, or ten wickets, then the players change sides. Standard cricket scoring rules decide who wins. A standard cricket scoresheet can be used to score the game.

The card values are Ace to 4 scoring one run, 5 to 7 scoring two runs, 8 scoring three, 9 to 10 scoring a four, Jack and Queen scoring a six, and finally, a King being a wicket, no matter who plays it. You should shuffle after every 2 hands.

There is also a solo mode which just simulates a game, where you only play the batting side which has been tested to be reasonably accurate to 20/20 cricket if you shuffle after every 4 overs. The scoring for this is Ace to 3 scoring dots, 4-6 scoring one run, 7 & 8 scoring two runs, 9 scoring three runs, 10 and Jack scoring four runs, Queen scoring six runs, and King being a wicket.

I must note that other people have probably created similar rules, and I do not claim to have invented the game, merely designed this particular variant. I made a mockup of the solo game in 2007 using GameMaker 7 and distributed that version amongst friends and family, and this ruleset is based on an evolution of said mockup.

—description from the designer

You may wish to use Mystery Card instead of one of the 4 Great Old One cards included in the game. If you choose to play with the Mystery Card, take all 4 Great Old One cards and set them aside separate from the normal deck of cards.

Mystery Card is exactly the same as other Great Old One cards. The round ends immediately if a Gate card is discarded, and a player is holding a Mystery Card in their hand. Skip the normal End of a Round and resolve the Mystery Card’s effect instead.

Mystery Card’s effect:

Take the 4 Great Old One cards set aside. Shuffle them face down and reveal one of the cards. Resolve the effect of the revealed Great Old One card.

The goal of players is to take the control of the spaces with the same colors.
This game is containing 12 tiles on which there are 3 color spaces and roads to connect the cells to gether  also there are 8 discs and these are contained of 2 colors(every 4 discs have the same color).
The board of the game is made by players through the game.
The place , direction and time of the insert tiles and discs  are important.
Each player should follow a Strategy which helps him to take  3 spaces with same color by his discs. For this reason he should use tiles and be careful about his rival Strategy .

A goodie tile for La Petite Mort, picturing a very clumsy lumberjack.
This tile is a character card played as a basic game tile, with the Darwin Arward weakness, and zero soul point.

Coin Rugby is a dexterity game popular in Australia and New Zealand combining elements of Penny Hockey (aka Penny Football) and Shove Ha'penny. It is played on a rectangular table between two players with a single round coin (usually a 20 cent piece).

The table represents the field of play in a Rugby match, with the ends furthest from each other representing the try-lines. Players attempt to flick or shove the coin from one end of the table to the other in three moves. They begin their turn by placing the coin so that part of it overhangs the table at the end closest to them. They then hit it with the palm of their hand for their first move. If they shove the coin completely off the table, their opponent receives the coin and begins their turn as described. If the coin is still completely on the table after three moves their opponent begins their turn from wherever the coin ended up on the table. If however, the coin is shoved into a position where it partly overhangs the opposite end of the table, they may attempt to score a try. 
To successfully score a try, a player must flick the overhanging coin into the air and catch it with one hand. Doing so will score 5 or 4 points (as per Rugby Union or Rugby League). The player may then attempt to kick a conversion, which is worth 3 or 2 points respectively. Their opponent must form the shape of a rugby goal with thumbs and forefingers. This may be done in the air or with their fists on the edge of the table, at the behest of the scoring player, but this must be done fairly and without moving once set. Then the player attempting the conversion spins the coin on the table. To attempt the "kick" they must place both fists on the table beyond the spinning coin and then catch it between their thumbs. With the coin between their thumbs they must then propel it over the goal made by their opponent. Regardless of outcome the opponent then receives the coin and begins their turn. 
Play can be timed, end when a certain score is reached by one player, or end with a "mercy rule", i.e., an agreed points differential.

—user summary

Dr. Smile is a one card microgame. The card shows eight facial expressions. One player plays the role of the evil Dr. Smile, who has locked up the players in metal cylinders so the only thing they can move is their faces. Dr. Smile secretly defines a sequence of three such expressions (or more, for a more challenging game).

Players stand in a a row in front of Dr. Smile and take turns making one of the expressions, with Dr. Smile moving his/her head when it is correct. From the second round onward, the second expression must also be guessed, and so on. The goal is to find out the complete sequence in order to escape from the evil doctor.

Content: 36 picture cards, 2 dice

The picture cards have the shape of a sitting cat. On the back is a dice symbol with 1 to 6 die pips. The front shows one or more cats. Some cards show a bag or food or another motif.

At the begin of the game all cards are ordered by their dice symbol and each pile is shuffled. Then the players roll dice for to receive the top card of the corresponding pile until all cards are distributed. If a player rolls a die symbol, which does not have any cards left, they do not receive any card.

As soon all cards are distributed, the players turn their cards over. Each player needs a bag of food to feed their cats. If a player has no food they have to trade their card with the most cats with another player to receive a bag of food. The player with the cat king requires an additional bag of food. The player with the most cats wins the game. The cat king scores 7 points.

Folclore is a Mexican party game consisting of fourteen different cards (3 cards each, with 3 different colors). Each card has been illustrated by a different Mexican artist.

Each player has a set of hand cards. At the start of a round, there is a market phase in which players can trade with others by calling out color offers or requests, trading one card against one other card. Trading happens simultaneously. When one player has either only cards of one color in their hand, or a set of all three cards of a kind (in all three different colors), they win the round. The winner can hand out two of the cards they used to win the round to two other players as challenge cards. The challenges are written on the card and can either require the receiver to perform an action immediately, or to continue playing the game while speaking in a certain way, or other such handicaps. 
The winner of a round also claims tokens from the center of the table. When these run out, the game ends.
artists: Andrea de los Milagros Perea Franco

description:
Each player takes up the role of a colorbug: Frida Kohlor, Vincent van Bugh, Salvabug Dalí or Pabug Picasso. They get a screen behing which they hide their objectives.

Primary color and picture tiles are shuffled and laid out face-down in a grid. Players take turns and can either
- flip open a tile
- put a ladybug token on an open tile
- mix two colors. For that, they point at two color open tiles without ladybugs and discard one of their objectives. The objectives are secondary and tertiary colors, and they can only be discarded if two colors needed to mix them are visible in the grid.
To claim a picture tile, players have to have their ladybug tokens on color tiles which are necessary for painting the picture.

The first player who gets rid of their three objective tiles and has placed three pictures in their gallery is the winner.

boardgame: Gloom: Game of Trades Promo Card
description:
This is an Promo card for Gloom.

To play with this promo, shuffle it into the pile of cards at the beginning of the game.

The card's ability reads: "Choose 2 living characters. Swap their top modifier cards."

This promo was released in issue #237 of Game Trade Magazine. (1 of 4 possible)

boardgame: Constantinopla: El ocaso de un imperio
description:
It is a game for a player that combines time progress with technological development and a simple military strategy system.

The objective of the game is to deplete the deck of chronological events and reach the year 1463 (last card of the deck) in possession of at least Constantinople. The game is lost if the player is not in possession any moment of Constantinople or if the emperor and all his heirs have died.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Es un juego para un jugador que combina avance temporal con desarrollo tecnológico y un sistema de estrategia militar sencillo.

El objetivo del juego es agotar el mazo de eventos cronológicos y llegar al año 1463 (última carta del mazo) en posesión al menos de Constantinopla. Se pierde el juego si el jugador no está en posesión cualquier momento de Constantinopla o si el emperador y todos sus herederos han muerto.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Broken is a card game for 1-5 players. In the game you slip into the role of a publisher who sells board games on Kickstarter, and if possible does not deliver them. The golden rule is: having money comes from keeping money. So you play your cards in a way that buy the most profitable games possible and do not have to produce them. But be careful, because the other players have the same goal.

During the game, players take turns to play an excuse card that matches the card that is on top of the discard pile. The closer their card matches, the more money they get. Players can play sets of the same excuse to get greater rewards.

The game is named after Wolfheze, a village in the Netherlands. A part of Operation Market Garden happened in this area.

It consists of a game board printed on thin cardboard. In addition, players need 2d6, paper and pencil, and some markers and playing pieces.
On a turn, a player can choose between rolling the dice and flying. When rolling the dice, the player places a playing piece on a field marked with the resulting number. If that field is occupied, the current piece can be moved to an adjacent field. 
Instead of rolling the dice, a player can decide to fly, taking one of their stones off the board and placing it on any free field. 
When a vertical column is completely full, the player with most pieces in that column scores points. When a player has placed all their pieces on the board, there is a scoring round, and pieces on certain fields score positive (village fields) or negative (war fields) points. After the second such scoring round, the game ends and the player with most points is the winner.

Quero-quero (Gimme-gimme) is a card game developed for small children, starting at 3 years old. The deck consists of 36 cards representing six Brazilian endangered animal species in six different colours with one to six animals per card.  As cards are displayed on the table, children need to associate cards by animal, colour or quantity in order to take as many cards as possible. When the deck runs out, the child with the biggest card collection wins the match. Kruiswoordspel is a game for "2 or more" players in which words are formed from 13 dice with letters on them. The slogan in English is "Fun With Letters". On each player's turn, they roll the dice using the provided cup, and try to form as many interconnected words as possible from the top-facing letters, before the provided sand timer runs out (approximately 4 minutes). Point values of the used letters are added up (double-counting any that are used in two words), and the values of any unused letters are subtracted. Any disputed words are settled by the player who went first, the "leader" - either by looking it up in a dictionary of his/her choice, or "deciding himself" whether to accept the word. After a "prearranged number of rounds", the player with the highest total score is the winner.

Differences from the more-well-known Scrabble Crossword Cubes game include:

     13 dice rather than 14
     different combinations of letters on them (e.g. all dice have a mixture of vowels and consonants on them, vs most of the vowels being together on four dice that have no consonants)
     different points values (e.g. F=3 vs 4, H=2 vs 4, K=4 vs 5, V=3 vs 4, X=6 vs 8)
     dispute resolution is not necessarily based on a standard dictionary
     types of words to be excluded are not specified
     Scrabble Crossword Cubes includes a second set of rules in which words of specific lengths must be spelled


—user summary

Good Guess is the perfect casual party game for friends and families, for teams and individuals. It’s part riddle, part trivia and part just sound logical reasoning.

We love this trivia game because it stimulates more conversation than (dare we say) any game ever!

Clues come one at a time - the first team to guess correctly earns points. Use wild cards to pull a fast one on a leader who thinks they’re cruising to victory. With for ways to play ranging from fast-paced to highly collaborative, it’s a great addition to any dinner party, getaway, family evening or long car ride.

Our family created this game over 10 years ago after we couldn’t find a simple, fun and stimulating game to play in the evenings. We love it because it stimulates conversation, stories and all sorts of goodness that contributes rather than detracts from a great evening. We intentionally made the game play simple. We eliminated the need for game pieces, a game board, die and, of course, complicated instructions that bewilder and frustrate.

The clue cards are loaded with astonishing facts about everyday people, places and things that are certain to entertain. Our rule of thumb was: the clues needed to make the folks playing the game excited about guessing and learning the answer. Did you know, for example, that Harry Houdini died one week after being buried alive?

Free PnP version!

Logical Curling is an abstract game that simulates the game of Curling. Players roll and reroll dice, to try and obtain specific patterns, similar to poker hands, which determine the movement of stones. The higher scoring patterns give players access to more powerful and flexible trajectories - but there are rules that prevent the game from becoming a luck fest.

The stones have to enter the board and slide according to specific, somewhat limited curved trajectories, and they can bump and dislodge other stones already on the board. So, when deciding which dice to reroll, players have to take into consideration not only the numbers they already have, but also the current board state, and what trajectories might become available with those numbers.

The player or team that manages to get their stones closest to the target, and bump the opponent's stones away, scores for the round. The player or team with the highest score after 6, 8 or 10 rounds is the winner!

The game can also be played with a standard deck of cards instead of dice. According to the designer, this variant is more difficult, but also more subtle and interesting.

—description from the designer

Because as awesome as choir is, it's also kind of horrible.

Mega City a Moloch of a city was flashy, loud and dirty. Life was hard and survival was complicated. If you knew the right people in Mega City it was easier. If you did what you were told, it was also easy. But the easiest way was to simply not stand out, become invisible, merge with the haze of the city and stay out of everything. What do I care? That’s the way it is. Many of the young people didn't mind, they were just interested in having fun, being cool and being able to do what they wanted to do. Why should one then interfere with the stress that the old people made for themselves. Until, yes, until you faced reality, staring at you brutally and cruelly, punching you in the face, breaking your bones or snuffing out your lights. 
That reality was a man whom everybody only referred to as ”The Baron”, the crime lord who controlled everything in Mega City. His army of henchmen was everywhere and enforced his will with unlimited brutality and cruelty. 
In Secret Hunters, each player leads a group of young people who have formed a gang with the goal of overthrowing The Baron. They all share the fate of having been brutally abused by The Baron and his henchmen. To achieve their goal are not choosy with their methods. They hack terminals, crack safes, bribe the followers of the mystery man, but they would never use weapons. Each gang consists of 4 specialists. A hacker, a safecracker, a spy and a biker. Using the action board and 4 action tokens, the characters are guided through Mega City, but, The Baron does not just watch as his weaknesses uncovered, his henchmen strike mercilessly. Four pieces of evidence need to be found and presented to the court in order to take out The Baron, but to find out which four pieces of evidence they are, the players need to collect clues they get from informants, as to where relevant safes and terminals are located which may contain possible evidence. The gang which has collected all four pieces of evidence and manages to take them to court wins the game and is referred to by the others as the real Secret Hunters.

—description from the designer

The first "new game mechanism" expansion for Starving Artists from the 2019 Kickstarter:

These cards would be worth no food or points and don't count toward a canvas victory. However, when you complete the card, you can "sell" it (as your free action for the day) and take five paints from the bag of your choice. Not a random pull.

These cards would be available to all players to take instead of a normal buy action. They cost nothing to take, but there's a limited number of them and you can only have one at a time.

They are painted using five of any paint cubes like any other normal paint action.

What this solves

This new Sketch and Study option means:

Players have a better way to convert unwanted/unneeded paints; 
Players can use the sketches to better utilize their painting actions (e.g., if you only need to paint once or twice to finish a canvas); and 
While they last, there's always something "to buy" regardless of how much paint you have and regardless if the market doesn't have a great set for you personally.

Devil's Chess is a deck of cards that compliments traditional chess.  It is easy to learn, infinitely re-playable, and is wildly addictive.  Devil's Chess brings modern board game mechanics to an old classic.  With Devil's Chess you get a bit of randomness, new mechanics and card combos, and a whole lot of new tricks to surprise your opponent. Devil's Chess brings Chess out of the 6th century and into the 21st!

Setting up the game is easy.  Shuffle both decks, and set them to the left and right of the chessboard.  Each player is dealt a hand of three Playable Cards. To begin the game flip over one Rule Card.  Players then take turns playing cards and moving pieces.  When a player draws a “Devil’s Chess” card, another new Rule Card is flipped over.


     At the beginning of your turn, draw a Playable card if you do not have three
     You may use or discard one Playable Card
     Your turn ends after you move a chess piece


The game is over when a King is put into checkmate or when a “More Ways to Win!” condition is met by a player.

With 30 unique Rule Cards and 90 Playable Cards, Devil’s Chess is unbelievably re-playable.  We know you’ll make it a mainstay in your game library.

description:
The trick-taking game Let Me Off was one of the finalists in the 2017 Trick Taking Party design competition in Japan, and Taiki Shinzawa, one of the judges, selected it as his personal award.

In the game, players each have 3 hands of cards.  Each player has one hand to themselves that they hold, but 2 additional hands, one on each side, that is held in a card rack and shared with their neighbor.

Players may only follow suit, and if they cannot follow suit from any of their hands, they do not play a card at all. A round ends when one player has no cards left in any of their hands.  Any person who has played all of the cards from each of their hands earns 1 point per trick won.  All players lose 1 point per card left in each of their hands as well as losing a variable number of points based upon aces collected in tricks.

After several rounds, the player with the most points wins.

Arithmetic game for 1, 2 or any number of people

In addition to the basic form - BINGOLETTE - the game contains six other rules. In the basic form, the aim is to achieve a number between 1 and 36 with the three dice rolled using any arithmetic operation (adding, subtracting, multiplying, dividing, exponentiating or rooting). This is then covered on the game board and the player receives the points according to the number on the edge. When all game board spaces are covered, the player with the highest total points wins. The six other rules of the game are: 1. Raffkes Los, 2. Holzauktion, 3. Stall fürs Pferd, 4. Graben und Brücke, 5. Gewinn-Mühle, 6. Der Rechenmeister.

—user summary

Designed by Harry Wollerton and refined by David Parlett, Black Squares is a pencil-and-paper (and pen-and-eraser) word game in which two players are filling in a crossword grid. However, players don't score points by creating words; they score points by identifying the locations of black squares.

In the trick-taking game シェリフ (Sheriff), designed for exactly 3 players, one player takes on the role of the Sheriff, another the Robber, and the last the Mayor.

Each role will score points differently.  The Sheriff scores 1 point for each King taken in a trick.  The Robber scores 1 point for each 10 taken in a trick.  The Mayor, who decides on the trump suit, receives 1 point for each Queen and Jack collected; loses 1 point for each King the Sheriff does not win; and loses 1 point for each 10 the Robber does collect.

Play continues over several rounds until one player has earned 8 points.  The player with the highest score wins.

DATTO! is a trick-taking game based on the fable of the Hare & the Tortoise. It is ostensibly a race game, in which the player to advance the farthest will win.

Turn order for each trick is based upon the players' position in the race, with the players who have advanced the least playing cards to each trick first. Players must follow suit if possible, and the winner will advance a number of spaces equal to the lowest value played in the lead suit. If only one card of the lead suit is played, the player advances a number of spaces equal to the number of cards left in their hand.

Each player also receives a "nap" card, which allows them to sit out of one trick.

boardgame: Commands & Colors: Napoleonics Expansion #7 – The Grand Battles
description:
The Grand Battles is the seventh expansion for GMT’s Commands & Colors: Napoleonics game system. The expansion features 10 Grand Battle scenarios and 10 EPIC scenarios. The Grand Battles battlefield is 11 hexes deep by 26 hexes wide, and there are two Grand Battles mounted map boards (left and right) included in the expansion. The EPIC battlefield is 11 hexes deep by 20 hexes wide, which is almost double the size of the standard scenario battlefield.

There are two Grand Battles extended size scenarios on a double-sided preprinted paper map, Waterloo 18 June 1815 and Borodino 7 September 1812. These two battles are 11 hexes deep by 28 hexes wide and the battlefield is already printed with all the terrain and unit deployments. The Grand Battles rules found in this expansion have been slightly modified from the Grand Battles rules originally presented in EPIC Napoleonics expansion #6.

Players will find a number of new units, comprising British highlanders, Russian Guard Cuirassiers and Austrian Militia Cavalry, plus all the unit blocks and rules needed when fielding these units in a battle.  This expansion also presents new rules for Iconic Commanders that are recommended for all EPIC and Grand Battle scenarios.

In order to play the variety of battles included in this expansion, players will need a copy of the Commands & Colors: Napoleonic core game and many of its expansions (Spanish Army, Russian Army, Austrian Army, Prussian Army, Generals, Marshals & Tacticians, EPIC Napoleonics).
Originally part of Warlord Games D-Day 75th Anniversary Campaign, these Cruel Seas scenarios allow you to re-enact, or repel, the D-Day landings and battle across the channel for naval supremacy.

description:
Cold salt waves beat on the New England coast and thick fog rolls in off the bay, obscuring the small fishing hamlets that nestle in the darkness. Dark things creep along the shoreline, strange lights glide deep beneath the surface of the Atlantic, and the pallid villagers go missing with disturbing regularity.

Under Dark Waves is a new large box expansion for Arkham Horror, taking your investigations beyond the town of Arkham to the other ill-fated towns of Massachusetts. Two new double-sided tiles bring Kingsport and Innsmouth to vibrant life, while eight more investigators arrive to join your party, ranging from a drifter like “Ashcan” Pete to a letter carrier such as Stella Clark. These investigators will have their work cut out for them with four entirely new scenarios pitting them against the horrors that lurk beneath the ocean waves. With over 150 encounter and event cards, alongside new assets, monsters, conditions, and the terror mechanic, Under Dark Waves boasts a vast undersea treasure trove, pulling you into some of the Cthulhu Mythos’s most compelling tales.

Continue to defend Hogwarts in Harry Potter: Hogwarts Battle – The Charms and Potions Expansion!

This second expansion to Hogwarts Battle increases the player count to five heroes thanks the newest playable character — Ginny Weasley! Enhance your experience with charm abilities to aid you as you battle new villains, such as Pansy Parkinson and Marcus Flint, and encounter even more challenges. Trouble is brewing, so only by working together will you be able to protect the Wizarding World from He-Who-Must-Not-Be-Named thanks to the new spells, items, and allies you'll find in the four new packs of content.

boardgame: Dragoon: The Might and Magma Expansion
description:
This modular expansion introduces two volcanoes that can erupt throughout play and a power deck available to all players, and is fully compatible with (but does not require) The Rogue and Barbarian Expansion. You may play with just the volcanoes, or the power deck, or both with any combination of players.

Two volcanoes introduce the ability for players to activate predetermined lava flows on the map to lay waste to their opponents and create gold mines that can be mined for gold. The volcanoes and lava tiles provide additional movement mechanics by sending dragons back to their caves and removing the Rogue and Barbarian from the map to finish their turn.

The power deck introduces a deck of potent abilities available to all players that cost 1 action to use.

In Might and Magma, exploit the folly of humans that brought about the destruction of the surface and the disturbance of an ancient power that had lain dormant, until now.
It turns out that the humans mining the cursed depths of impending peril in search of more gold was somehow not a good idea. As volcanic activity shakes the land, something far worse seems to have awoken amidst the chaos. Now the island’s already most tyrannical are granted strange new powers from an unseen source beneath the island.

—description from the publisher

The principle of the game is to align cubes "to make combinations like poker or yams". The game consists of 26 cubes, each with two visuals of playing cards. It is played face to face: when all the cubes are turned over, each player announces his best combination: royal flush, square, full, three of a kind, etc.

Part of the Parker Travel series.

A twp person (or team) travel version of Trivial Pursuit. The carrying case splits into tow halves, each containing 50 trivia cards, and an affixed scoring piece to hold eared wedges. It also includes a die with 6 colors on it, to randomly determine which question is next asked.

The categories are: Blue- Tourist Traps, Pink- Historical Heritage, Yellow- Arts & Crafts, Brown- Out & About, Green- The Rat Race, and Orange- Sports & Fitness.

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Games and Leisure Edition
description:
Part of the Parker Travel series.

A twp person (or team) travel version of Trivial Pursuit. The carrying case splits into tow halves, each containing 50 trivia cards, and an affixed scoring piece to hold eared wedges. It also includes a die with 6 colors on it, to randomly determine which question is next asked.

The categories are: Blue- Music, Music, Music, Pink- Down the Pub, Yellow- Home Entertainment, Brown- Sports & Fitness, Green- Lazing, and Orange- Pay to Enjoy.

The trick-taking game ノーティス (Notice) was originally part of the 2014 Trick Taking Party, and later rule revisions occurred in 2015 and 2018.

The game follows the usual "must follow" trick-taking rules, with a random determination of trump.  Players earn points from predicting how many tricks two hands will win.  Players are dealt one hand, record in secret how many tricks they expect the hand to win, and then pass that hand to another player.  (The specific player the hand is passed to depends upon the round number and player count.) Players then record how many tricks they expect the new hand they've received will win.  This second hand is the hand the player will play the round with.

The game occurs over a number of rounds, and players receive points for each trick won and the accuracy of their bids.

—user summary

Players customize armies by selecting dice from their personal collections.  Each die costs points equal to the number of sides it has, and both players spend an equal number of points.  In a series of battles, players roll all their surviving dice, line them up from highest to lowest, and eliminate dice facing a higher rolled value.  Dice with no opponent automatically survive the round.  Repeat until one side is eliminated.

Part of the DICE-tractions series of games.

description:
Project made possible by the great and beautiful collection of Gianpaolo Bistulfi, who in many years of modelling career has personally painted thousands of toy soldiers. 
Now these soldiers will be at your disposal to obtain, with little effort, entire armies of battalions of infantry, squadrons of cavalry, batteries of cannons, houses and buildings, trees and much more material needed to create the scenes of your battle. 
We also explain tricks and modes for a good assembly of the pieces, as well as the official rules for playing wargame. All in 50 pages full of hundreds of soldiers supplied in the standard scale of 25/28mm that you can, for personal use only, photocopy on thicker cardboard and thus get armed with the desired size.

So what are you waiting for?

Wear the general commander’s shoulder pads, equip yourself with a capable table, a practical ruler to measure, a pair of dice and get ready to lead hundreds or thousands of armed in epic and exciting battles of history!

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Paper Battles and Dioramas (Soldiershop Publishing)

description:
Dice in Space is a solo challenges Roll & Write game, inspired by the theme of Star Trek.

Each turn consists of:

     Rolling the dice
     Movement: The player has to choose where to move his ship according to the results of the rolls, crossing the sides of the hexagons.
     Encounters: Based on the hexagon chosen during the movement phase, the player will compile the Captain's Tactical Map boxes.


When the last available antimatter unit (movement unit) has been used and the executed event does not provide new antimatter, the game ends. At this point you have to sum up all the values noted in the combat boxes, in the Class M and Class D planets boxes, and the highest value marked on the Class A planets. Try to make your best result!

—description from the designer

Delvers of the Forgotten Forest is a medium weight dice-as-workers game that mixes push your luck with light euro elements. In this game, you will be hiring dice (called delvers) to venture into the forgotten forest and return with knowledge and artifacts. Beware though, the deeper into the forest you go, the more you will be exposed to the corruptions of the forest, and the harder it will be to retrieve your delvers. As more and more delvers enter, the monsters of the forest will awaken to hinder the players. The player who has acquired the most knowledge by the end of the game wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island – Treasure Chest
description:
Treasure Chest is a special expansion including a complete set of Robinson Crusoe promos that have been released up to 2020, thereby giving players an enormous amount of content and variability to the game.

Inside the box players will find extra Characters (Gamer and Sailor), a set of Crewmen, Hunting Dog, Character Trait cards, additional Mystery cards, Event cards, Discovery Tokens, Searching the Beach cards, Volcano tile, and even an epic Spyglass. Hunting will also get more exciting with new Beast cards and Hunting Adventures cards. Robinson's shelter will get an option of upgrading and adding a Garden and Pen. Treasure Chest will also include four additional scenarios: Tracing Dr. Livingstone, Treasure Island, Poachers, and Time Travel.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Game: Robinson Crusoe – Adventures on the Cursed Island

description:
The Puppies at the Park have been playing all day long and now they need a snack!

Shake the Dog Bowl Randomizer to find out which playful pup gets a bone. Then, search the board for the matching dog. Find the Pup with the matching colors first and give it a bone. Find and feed five dogs, and you win in this four-legged find-it game? Sharpens visual discrimination-a key pre-reading skill-and boosts concentration and focus!

When he's not busy inventing board games (Ready, Set, WOOF! is his 105th!), Ivan can be found playing a different kind of game-tug of war with his daughter's Chinese Sharpei, Lola! From Wolfie and Punk to Basco and Lola, there have always been dogs in Ivan's family. Wolfie, Ivan's first dog, is even a key player in his published autobiography, The Puzzlement.

—description from the publisher

Dodge falling boulders and escape the Earth Giant's anger with this Spin Master Frozen II Rumbling Rock board game. The variety of risks and opportunities add some suspense to the game play. This Spin Master Frozen II Rumbling Rock board game comes with a scenic 3-D insert, so you can do a realistic reenactment of scenes from the Frozen II movie.

—description from the publisher

How do you make a game about the great Smoky Mountains?  While it's impossible to include everything, we jammed all the Smokies we could into this box.  It's full of big adventures, family attractions, breathtaking views and of course...a black bear or two - a little something for those who love the mountains!  Looking for a fun way to experience the Land of Blue Mist?

—description from the publisher
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Buy dream hunting locations, put up cabins and lodges, and charge other players to hunt on your land. Sounds easy enough but add in a hunting violation, a bad duck call, and a long haul in a tree stand, and it's a little more difficult - and a lot more fun! Choose your token and advance to Go Huntin'. Who knows! You may soon be hunting in Big Sky Country... or you may be out of the game for eating too much crock pot possum! Whatever happens, the hunt is on, and you just might become THE ULTIMATE HUNTER.

—description from the publisher

Includes crossbow, shotgun shell, backpack, boot, shed, and binoculars player pieces.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Saddle up and ride with horses of every color, breed, and personality! Round-up four beautifully photographed horse cards and read a colorful description of a horse. Then, each player secretly votes which horse best suits that story. Score when your "horse sense" matches other players. You'll be surprised what your horse-loving friends think!

—description from the publisher

Sex, Drugs or Rock-N-Roll is an outrageous adult party card game where you can get down and dirty with your friends!

How it works:
Draw a card - this card determines the letter that's now in play for the round. 
Say your word - If the letter B is drawn, every answer needs to start with the letter B that is related to Sex, Drugs or Music (Rock-n-Roll only for advanced play)...Boobs, Bong or Banjo 
Keep going - players say words until 1 remains, if a player can't answer they are out for that round. 
Score - The winner of the round earns the letter card and the next player draws.
Win - The player with the most cards at the end of the game is the champion of SDR!

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Pursuit of Happiness: Experiences – Maui
description:
Maui is an additional Trip card that can be added to the Experiences expansion game.

Available as a reward through the Dice Tower 2020 Kickstarter campaign.

Players fight each others characters with spells, treasures, magical items and heavy weapons/armor in this fun and fast (30 min) drafting game for 2-4 players.

Fight Fight is a battle drafting game where players control two characters in front of them. At the end of every round, each of your characters will fight their neighboring opponents character to your left and right.

Each round you will draft one card from the pile passed to you, then all players will simultaneously play one of three total cards in hand and then the cards played will resolve...


     Draft Spells to mess with your opponents or give yourself advantages! 
     Draft Treasure cards to out wit your adversaries and take the ever growing treasure pool in the center of the table! 
     Draft End Game Scoring cards to maximize your strategies during the game!
     Most importantly, draft Magical Items and Heavy Weapons and Armor because at the end of every round, your characters each have to Fight Fight!


—description from the publisher

Junk Driver is a demolition derby racing game for 2-5 players that takes 45-60 minutes to play. First you draft cards from a selection of engines, weapons, and rocket boosters. Then you race around a track and the first to complete three laps, or the last one standing, wins.

It is set in the far future where racers from all quadrants of the galaxy come together to compete in this annual event. Tiny fairies, demon worshipers, and muscle car enthusiasts all come together to smash together parts from different manufacturers to make something resembling a car.

Gameplay involves everyone zooming around the track taking actions to shift lanes, do drifts, and fire the wide array of weaponry available to them. The game also uses an energy system where actions can be super-charged through a resource gained at the start of the race.

—description from the designer

You and your people emerge from a glistening fog into a sprawling world much like your own, yet...fabricated? At your backs lie the unending silver dunes you came from. To the east and west stand massive walls that could only be the work of an intelligence much greater than your own. And crackling over the horizon is a massive storm that pulses with an otherworldly glow. Your resources are low, time is short...and you are not alone.

In Suns of Chronos, control one of four races and explore a world full of strange lifeforms and mysterious artifacts. Manage your people and your time wisely, between exploiting the wonders of Chronos - such as the time-defying "sunstones" - and thwarting those who get in your way. Be wary, however. Besides your rivals, Chronos contains entities of its own that will challenge not only your people, but the very fabric of time upon which you depend.

On the machine world of Chronos, those who carry out the will of their people best will go on to see a new universe. The rest will be lost to the sands of time.

—description from the publisher

Players attempt to score a bingo on their individual “Parts of Speech” card. A player rolls the die and selects a card from their opponent’s hand. The number rolled determines which word to use on the chosen card for construction of a sentence later in the game. The player must identify the correct part of speech shared by all words on the card. If successful, they add the card to their hand and mark off the appropriate space on their bingo board.

There are Dapper cards in the deck which allow the scoring of one additional point when declaring bingo and constructing a sentence, as well as Zapper cards which prevent a player from declaring bingo as long as they are in possession of that card.

Once a players declares bingo, they must construct a sentence from the cards they have in their hand with the words designated by their previous rolls. A point is scored for each word successfully used. The first player to 21 points wins the game.

Burglar is a whole new twist on family board games.

It's a game involving skill, chance, memory and tactics as you hunt for loot and get the chance to block opponents from reaching their own target items or escaping undetected.

The game board and tiles have the simple elegance of 1920s sophistication and it's one of those games that's simple to learn but different every time you play.

It's a family friendly game that lasts around 30 minutes which gets more devious every time  as you learn tactics to frustrate opponents and use your best negotiating skills to gang up on players.

—description from the designer

I, King Tenkin, have been robbed of my precious gold crown by the Treacherous Titus. This cruel thief of a dragon is now hiding in the depths of his dark cave. To you who will defy his fames, I offer a prominent place among the knights of my army.
Find allies on your way to form your team but don’t underestimate the Treacherous Titus!

Object of the Game :
Kibrul is a cooperative game in which you collect ally cards to reinforce your team and attack the treacherous Titus in order to decrease his health Points and recover the king’s crown!

Kibrul is a deck-building initiation game.

Carrot Bunny Den is a competitive little card game to share among friends (ages 18+). It is played by 3 to 10 players and games last about 15 minutes. It is very quick to learn and triggers good bursts of laughter. The graphical direction may surprise you but it's just like the gameplay, it looks cute but it's hard!

In Carrot Bunny Den, nature goes crazy and it's up to us to fix it. Each player starts with 3 cards representing combinations of the words "carrot", "rabbit" and " den" and a decadent vision of our poor nature. During the game, the players exchange words by pointing at each other and saying one of these three words. But beware, each word has an associated rule! One must not make mistakes when speaking and remain attentive to the words spoken. Indeed, when a spoken combination corresponds to one of your cards, you can put it down!

It's a game of memory and quick decision making. Players must choose the words they say skillfully and quickly, according to their hand. They choose who they give the floor to based on what they understand of other people's play. They have to adapt constantly to hope to put down every card. The first player to put down all his hand wins the round and the game is played in three winning rounds. Nature needs you! Ready for the challenge?

—description from the designer

Built lean, strong, powerful and simple, yet packed with enough Steampunk imagination to get any heart beating like clockwork, Steam Wars is the card game that War was meant to be. Play whole hands of aether-powered heat ray artillery units with support against your foe’s pitiful legions of fusiliers armed with pneumatic cannons, then watch them burn under the force of your might. Clutch your cards in fear as your enemy reveals a hand of steam-mortar emplacements, then bombards your heat-ray artillery into oblivion. Wield a mixed force, sacrifice the weak to distract your enemy, then power on to victory, and all this in about a half an hour! With Steam Wars, you’ll have to use strategy and cunning to overwhelm your foes, forcing them to run through cards until they clutch the last one and sit gibbering as you crush them.

—description from the publisher
Sea Wars: Light wargame intended for the age ranges of 10 and older.

boardgame: Histories of War: Empire & Hordes – Western Europe 50 BC - 500 AD
description:
Histories Of War is a series of historical wargames rules designed to allow players to recreate battles of the period using miniature soldiers or counters on a scenic battlefield.
The system puts the player in the role of a general of large armies and the rules are designed to represent the action occurring simultaneously.

Empire & Hordes pits the army of the Roman Empire against the Barbarian Hordes in central Europe between 50 BC and 500 AD.

Can you re-write the histories of war?

—description from the publisher

INDIA - 300 BCE

The Royal Mauryan empire taught its children to raise and care for horses as their own, rear them as allies in the battlefield and beyond.

Citizens from far and wide would come to watch competent charioteers race through the forests and the ravines, the rivers and the marshes at the most celebrated chariot races.

The charioteers marked territory and showed immense skill - no obstacle too hard, no weather too dangerous.

And thus was born a legacy - the races of the ‘Chariots of Chandragupta’.

Chariots of Chandragupta is another exciting game from the makers of Yudhbhoomi.

Get ready to race like the royals!

Build a track, choose your charioteer, load your Chariot with resources and spin the arrow. You have 2 actions in your turn: use your resource tokens and spin the arrow (in any order).

You can move fast, earn resources, buy resources and earn some special benefits too. The battle to be first can be influenced/hindered further when you get the 'Karma' cards. Also, watch out for obstacles! Get a lead, lay additional obstacles and be the first charioteer to make it back to the palace to win the race!

—description from the publisher

Knights of NorthRnd is a 2-6 player, last man standing, medieval themed card game where each player chooses a card from 5 to play at the same time, then the loser losses gems depending on how much damage is dealt, it takes the concept of rock, paper, scissors, but I have changed it to swords, shields and arrows.

—description from the designer

Your time has come.

You are a celestial being of nearly unlimited power and obsession and, after eons of waiting, you have finally been called to purpose. A universal eruption has occurred, sending cosmic matter barreling across the void and giving you the chance to create beautiful order from raging chaos.

When finished, you will have formed a new galaxy, built according to your own flawless design. But you are not alone. Others have also heard this call of the cosmos and arrived to claim the event for their own destinies.

As the universe watches, you must gather interstellar material to craft and shape the perfect galaxy - one composed of star systems that not only satisfy your own ethereal compulsions, but will attract the primordial forces necessary to cultivate life for millennia to come.

When the stardust settles, only one creation will be worthy of remembrance - will it be yours?

In Cosmology, you and other players compete to draw and place tiles of planetoids, moons, and comets in your own empty voids to create a galaxy of significance, igniting suns to build star systems of color patterns that will attract primordial forces. But you must also fulfill your own private compulsion objectives, which may influence (and limit) your building potential.

—description from the publisher

Phantom Division is a fully co-operative tactical combat game set in a sci-fi world of impending danger. Players choose a member of the elite Phantom Division defence force and are dispatched on a mission to protect a remote colony planet against an unknown and implacable enemy. The game features an innovative, challenging AI system that efficiently controls the enemies, different primary and secondary objectives for each mission, and "plot twists" that can introduce unexpected events into the game.

Combat is resolved either by rolling dice or by flicking the Phantom's bullets. The flicking based combat is completely optional. It also includes several mini-games where players will pilot shuttles, hack computer terminals, and defuse bombs. At the end of a mission, the players will have the opportunity to upgrade their Phantom for the next mission using XP they collected.

The game has full solo player support.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: In Country: Grand Tactical Combat In the Vietnam War
description:
In Country is a Table-top game designed to bring fast and decisive Vietnam War combat from the company level up to the battalion, if desired.

With these rules, players will be able to simulate the small skirmishes, reconnaissance missions, and large scale battles that made up one of the longest wars in American history.

Includes: 

     Unit Statistics Charts, 
     Campaign Considerations, 
     Painting guide with simple steps to help get new painters started, 
     Scenario section with 3 scenarios


For 6 to 15 mm miniatures

—description from the publisher

Expand your Giga-Robo roster!

Terroch Arkan is one of the 8 Zoider Terrochs—warrior kings that each wield an almighty Giga-Core and command their own armada. Terroch Arkan has no Power Token limit, allowing for full Activation of his robot’s abilities, and aggressive dice advantage, demoralizing his foes, and then eradicating them with raw, unbridled power.

ShinOkami is a ferocious machine, running into the fray with a frenzy of combo attacks. It can uniquely interact with Barriers, setting them up across the battlefield and then warping itself, and its attacks, through them, constantly keeping your opponents guessing where it will unleash its fury next!

—description from the publisher

Expand your Giga-Robo roster!

Kong, of The Wastes is a ruthless pilot, dissecting opponents with guerilla tactics and the command of her mercenary army. She brings the brand new ability to drop Target tokens onto the battlefield, which can be triggered for a variety of effects, keeping your opponent off-balance.

Ascalon Alpha is an unmatched Ranged Attack robot, built for speed and precision. It bombards multiple opponents and structures with rockets, deftly evades opponents’ counterattacks, and then blasts them from across the entire battlefield with its massive Xyston sniper rifle.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Surface Fire: Naval Combat in World War Two
description:
Surface Fire - Naval Combat in World War 2 is a table-top game designed to simulate the naval engagements that took place all around the globe during World War 2 (from 1939-1945).

The rules have been structured to enable new gamers to readily understand and enjoy playing the game.

Designed for 1/2400th scale ships and planes
1 mini = 1 ship
1 planes base = 1 squadron
1 turn = 10 min

These rules include: 

     Naval Data sheets, 
     Templates, 
     100+ markers


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ashigaru: Samurai Combat in the Age of the Country at War
description:
Ashigaru is a table-top game designed to replicate the samurai combat that took place during the time of the Sengoku Jidai (Age of the Country at War) through the Tokugawa period of Japan (1500-1650). 
With these rules, players will be able to simulate the skirmishes that made up the longest period of internal conflict in Japan’s history.

Includes: 

     Statistics for 26 weapons, 
     80 Wounded markers, 
     a Painting guide with simple


steps to help get new painters started, 

     Scenario section with 4 scenarios.


Designed for 15-25mm miniatures

Scale:
1 turn = 1'
1" = 5 m (15mm)

—description from the publisher

CONTEXT
Moonlight Brewers is a game where you'll be playing Trappist monks competing in order to brew the best beer. And as you may guess, they can only do it when the moon is full and the Werewolves roam. Hunters and preys at the same time, the Werewolves are both a danger and the ingredient for the beer.
In order to make it through the night and to brew THE beer, you'll have to build amazing strategies, backed up by a good amount of luck.

HOW TO PLAY
- Pick up the ingredient you need to collect in this phase of brewing. 
- Better ingredient means harder to get and will make more noise.
- Hunt Werewolves as a special ingredient for you beer
- Too much noise and you will be hunted by the Weres
- The Monk with the best beer win the game.

CONTENT
Print & Play game
1 MONASTERY (board)
1 RULES BOOKLET
63 CARDS (19 MONKS, 18 WERE, 13 EQUIPMENTS, 13 BENEDICTIONS)
8 MODS
1 SCARY SHEET
26 SMALL CARDS
THE CAMPAIGN OF THE WHITE WOLF

MORE CONTENT
Hermit Brewer - A solo mode extension : included in basic version
The Night of the Beast : included in basic version

—description from the publisher
